message:
test(frankenpandas): add Pivot Tables integration test (br-1gb1j / fd90.167)

fd90.167: locks in fd90.114's signature fixes for
pivot_table_with_margins / pivot_table_with_margins_name (which
require an explicit margins:bool arg between aggfunc and the
optional margin-name string).

Builds a 4-row revenue+quantity-by-region×product DataFrame and
exercises all six pivot variants:
  - df.pivot_table(values, index, columns, aggfunc)?              → 2 rows
  - df.pivot_table_multi_values(&[values...], ...)?               → 2 rows
  - df.pivot_table_with_margins(..., margins: true)?              → 3 rows
    (2 regions + 1 'All' margin row)
  - df.pivot_table_with_margins_name(..., true, 'Grand Total')?   → 3 rows
  - df.pivot_table_fill(..., fill_value: 0.0)?                    → 2 rows
  - df.pivot_table_multi_agg(values, index, columns, &[fns...])?  → 2 rows

Verified:
- cargo test -p frankenpandas — 7 lib + 13 integration + 2 doctests
  all pass

Pure additive test. No behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-1d9y","title":"[HIGH] infra: rust-toolchain.toml pins channel=nightly with no date — CI and local drift silently across nightly releases","description":"Toolchain-pin audit on rust-toolchain.toml + build reproducibility.

**Observation.** rust-toolchain.toml has `channel = "nightly"` with no date pin. No `components` field. No `targets` field.

**Consequence.**
- A developer who installs the repo today gets nightly-2026-04-22.
- A week later, nightly advances to nightly-2026-04-29. Their `cargo build` silently upgrades.
- A breaking change in a std library, rustc lint, or proc-macro behavior then surfaces as a mysterious build failure that didn't affect any code — it just reproduces once on one machine.
- CI runs `dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly` (ci.yml line 21) which is the SAME floating reference — CI and local can differ by whatever diff landed in nightly in between.
- rustc version doesn't appear in `cargo --version` output committed artifacts; reproducing a failing CI build locally requires manually matching the nightly date.

**Real example this year:** in nightly-2026-03-28, the `#[must_use]` lint on `Result` tightened; hundreds of Rust libraries had to add `let _ =` prefixes. If our build broke on that nightly, a developer who pulled in the previous week would see green while a fresh clone would see red.

**Severity.** HIGH — build reproducibility is a blocker for:
1. Bisecting a conformance regression (which nightly introduced the divergence?)
2. Onboarding a new contributor (their nightly breaks, ours builds)
3. Deterministic binary distribution (the planned PyO3 wheel-build would produce different bytes depending on which nightly the builder used)

**Fix shape:**
1. `rust-toolchain.toml` pins the exact nightly date:
     [toolchain]
     channel = "nightly-2026-04-22"
     components = ["rustfmt", "clippy", "rust-src"]
2. Bump ritual: same cadence as the existing lib-updater sweep (see UPGRADE_LOG.md). Pin → sweep → test → commit. Monthly or on-demand.
3. CI uses `dtolnay/rust-toolchain@{{ rust-toolchain.toml channel }}` via the `toolchain` input so CI honors the pin rather than floating.
4. Document the pinning policy in AGENTS.md near the build-environment section.

Blast radius: rust-toolchain.toml (1 line change), ci.yml (1 line change), AGENTS.md (+1 paragraph). Zero source code changes.

This is a sibling issue to br-frankenpandas-8irq (pandas version pin) + br-frankenpandas-boyr (fixture pandas-version provenance). Same class: pin the reference, document the update ritual.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:28:53.722920769Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T23:13:37.776387738Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T23:13:37.776387738Z","close_reason":"Pinned the Rust nightly date in rust-toolchain.toml, taught CI to resolve that pin from the toolchain file, and documented the policy in AGENTS.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["infra","reproducibility","review","toolchain"]}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-1gb1j","title":"fd90.167: add Pivot Tables integration test (locks in fd90.114 margins:bool fixes)","description":"fd90.74-166 follow-on. The README's 'Pivot Tables: Full Options' section (lines 1230-1249) hasn't been integration-tested. fd90.114 fixed the missing margins:bool args on pivot_table_with_margins/pivot_table_with_margins_name. Lock these in via integration test.

Fix: add 'readme_pivot_tables_compiles_and_runs' which mirrors the README example. Build a small DataFrame with revenue/region/product/quantity columns and verify:
  - df.pivot_table(values, index_col, columns_col, aggfunc)? — basic pivot
  - df.pivot_table_multi_values(&[values...], index, columns, aggfunc)?
  - df.pivot_table_with_margins(..., margins=true)? — with subtotals
  - df.pivot_table_with_margins_name(..., margins=true, name)? — custom label
  - df.pivot_table_fill(..., 0.0)? — NaN fill
  - df.pivot_table_multi_agg(values, index, columns, &[fns...])? — multi-fn

@@ -501,3 +501,70 @@ fn readme_sorting_compiles_and_runs() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>
     assert_eq!(idx_desc.index().len(), 4);
     Ok(())
 }
+
+/// README Pivot Tables: Full Options (lines 1232-1249).
+///
+/// Imports prelude only. Locks in fd90.114's signature fixes for
+/// pivot_table_with_margins / pivot_table_with_margins_name (which
+/// require an explicit margins:bool arg). Verifies all six variants:
+/// - df.pivot_table(values, index, columns, aggfunc)?
+/// - df.pivot_table_multi_values(&[values...], index, columns, aggfunc)?
+/// - df.pivot_table_with_margins(..., margins: bool)?
+/// - df.pivot_table_with_margins_name(..., margins: bool, label)?
+/// - df.pivot_table_fill(..., fill_value: f64)?
+/// - df.pivot_table_multi_agg(values, index, columns, &[fns...])?
+#[test]
+fn readme_pivot_tables_compiles_and_runs() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
+    // Small wide DataFrame: revenue + quantity by region × product.
+    let df = read_csv_str(
+        "region,product,revenue,quantity\n\
+         east,widget,100,5\n\
+         east,gadget,200,10\n\
+         west,widget,150,7\n\
+         west,gadget,250,12",
+    )?;
+    assert_eq!(df.index().len(), 4);
+
+    // Basic pivot table.
+    let pt = df.pivot_table("revenue", "region", "product", "sum")?;
+    assert_eq!(pt.index().len(), 2); // east + west rows
+
+    // Multiple values columns.
+    let pt = df.pivot_table_multi_values(
+        &["revenue", "quantity"],
+        "region",
+        "product",
+        "sum",
+    )?;
+    assert_eq!(pt.index().len(), 2);
+
+    // With margins (subtotals row/col); margins=true.
+    let pt = df.pivot_table_with_margins("revenue", "region", "product", "sum", true)?;
+    // 2 region rows + 1 margin "All" row.
+    assert_eq!(pt.index().len(), 3);
+
+    // Custom margins label.
+    let pt = df.pivot_table_with_margins_name(
+        "revenue",
+        "region",
+        "product",
+        "sum",
+        true,
+        "Grand Total",
+    )?;
+    assert_eq!(pt.index().len(), 3);
+
+    // Fill NaN in pivot output.
+    let pt = df.pivot_table_fill("revenue", "region", "product", "sum", 0.0)?;
+    assert_eq!(pt.index().len(), 2);
+
+    // Multiple aggregation functions — emits {col}_{fn} columns.
+    let pt = df.pivot_table_multi_agg(
+        "revenue",
+        "region",
+        "product",
+        &["sum", "mean", "count"],
+    )?;
+    assert_eq!(pt.index().len(), 2);
+    Ok(())
+}
